what_it_does: Implements a standalone, lazily-loaded cross-encoder reranker that re-scores and re-orders search candidates using a selectable transformer backend (jina-reranker-v3 or ms-marco-MiniLM), with a CLI for self-testing, ad-hoc reranking, and reranking a JSON candidate pool produced by an upstream search step.
observations: Backends are declared in a registry-is-truth dictionary RERANKERS keyed by short name, with hf_id, load_kind, and trust_remote_code flags.
The Reranker class loads model weights lazily on first .rerank() or .load(), defaults to CPU, and hides CUDA unless explicitly requested.
It supports two loader strategies: jina_listwise using AutoModel and the model's native .rerank() API, and seqcls_crossencoder using AutoTokenizer + AutoModelForSequenceClassification logits.

The public rerank() accepts candidates as strings or dicts, extracts text via a pluggable text_of callable, returns the original items annotated with rerank_score, orig_rank, and rank, sorted descending by score.
CLI subcommands include selftest (controlled probe expecting the most relevant doc to move to top), rerank (query + docs), and rerank-file (read query/candidates/k JSON and print before/after).
Environment overrides: COMPANY_RERANKER selects default backend; COMPANY_RERANK_DTYPE can override the default float32/bfloat16 choice for jina-v3.
standouts: Explicit architectural separation: the lean substrate venv stays free of torch/transformers; the heavy reranker runs in the existing ~/vllm-env.
Fail-loud design: unknown backend, empty candidates, or unimplemented load_kind raise rather than silently degrading.
Self-test probe is deliberately mis-ordered and asserts that reranking actually changes the top result to a relevant doc.
rerank-file acts as a clean stage-2 seam: substrate dumps candidates as JSON, this module scores them, preserving venv boundaries.
conventions: Heavy module-level docstring explaining purpose, design rationale, backends, and usage.
Type hints throughout; Optional, Sequence, Union, Callable from typing.
Default values drawn from environment variables where appropriate.
Lazy initialization to avoid loading multi-GB models at import time.
concerns: jina-v3 loading passes a `dtype` keyword to AutoModel.from_pretrained, but standard transformers expects `torch_dtype`; this may only work because of the custom Jina model signature.
The selftest only checks that the top result moved to one of two expected input positions, not that all relevant docs are ranked above irrelevant ones.
rerank-file assumes payload candidate distance is cosine distance when it prints cos~{1-distance:.3f}, which may be wrong if the upstream search uses a different distance metric.
No explicit handling if the jina .rerank() result keys differ from the expected document/relevance_score/index shape.
notes: A reusable, venv-isolated rerank stage with a registry of transformer backends and a CLI that verifies itself by reordering a deliberately scrambled probe.
questions: Does the custom Jina AutoModel actually accept `dtype=` rather than `torch_dtype=`?
What exact JSON schema does rerank-file expect from the substrate search output?
How is the reranker intended to be wired into the live substrate search path beyond the manual rerank-file stage?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and implementation align: it is a standalone, reusable rerank capability with lazy loading, backend registry, CPU default, fail-loud behavior, and a substrate-seam CLI.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns between lean search substrate and heavy inference runtime
intention_signals: The file is trying to make reranking a durable, model-agnostic Company capability that can be dropped between any embedding search and any consumer, while keeping the throwaway substrate lightweight and GPU-free by default.
novelty: The explicit two-venv seam (substrate dumps JSON candidates; reranker in torch env consumes them) and the registry-is-truth backend table with environment-driven dtype override.
contribution: A reusable, backend-pluggable rerank primitive plus a clean stage-2 CLI seam, so any search pipeline can add cross-encoder precision without coupling to a specific vector store or venv.
summary_for_embedding: company/ops/rerank.py is a standalone transformer-based reranker module with a registry of backends, lazy CPU-by-default loading, and CLI commands for self-testing and reranking JSON candidate pools.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to provide a reusable, venv-isolated rerank step that outlives the current substrate and can be wired into any two-stage retrieval pipeline.

symbols.does: Extracts the rerankable text from a candidate that is either a string or a dict.
symbols.description: Tries common text fields ('text', 'document', 'content', 'chunk', 'body') on dict inputs and falls back to str(item); used as the default text_of callable for Reranker.rerank.

symbols.does: Lazily-loaded, model-agnostic reranker class backed by the RERANKERS registry.
symbols.description: Encapsulates backend selection, lazy model loading, scoring, and reranking. Defaults to CPU and the backend named by COMPANY_RERANKER, and exposes load(), _score(), and rerank().

symbols.does: Validates the chosen backend and stores device and spec, without loading weights.
symbols.description: Raises ValueError for unknown backends; sets self.backend, self.spec, self.device, and lazy-load placeholders.

symbols.does: Loads the selected transformer model and tokenizer onto the configured device.
symbols.description: Imports torch, configures CPU thread count, optionally blanks CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES for CPU mode, then dispatches by load_kind to load either a Jina listwise model or a sequence-classification cross-encoder; records load time.

symbols.does: Returns a relevance score for each text in input order using the loaded backend.
symbols.description: For jina_listwise it calls the model's native rerank() and remaps sorted results back to input-order scores by index; for seqcls_crossencoder it tokenizes query+text pairs and returns logits as scores.

symbols.does: Re-orders candidates by relevance score and returns annotated results.
symbols.description: Loads the model if needed, extracts texts, scores them, sorts descending, optionally truncates to top_n, and returns dicts containing the original item, text, rerank_score, orig_rank, and rank.

symbols.does: CLI self-test that reranks a deliberately mis-ordered probe and checks the top result moves to a relevant doc.
symbols.description: Instantiates the chosen backend, reranks _PROBE_DOCS, prints before/after ordering, and returns non-zero if the top result did not move to one of the expected relevant positions.

symbols.does: CLI ad-hoc rerank of a query against one or more doc strings.
symbols.description: Creates a Reranker for the selected backend/device and prints the re-ordered docs with scores and original ranks.

symbols.does: CLI stage-2 rerank of a candidate pool stored as JSON.
symbols.description: Reads a JSON file containing query, candidates, and optional k, reranks using dict text extraction, and prints before/after lists including cosine-like distance and address metadata.

symbols.does: Helper that pulls an address value from candidate metadata.
symbols.description: Looks inside candidate.metadata.address for dict inputs; returns None otherwise; used only for display in cmd_rerank_file.

symbols.does: Argparse entry point dispatching selftest, rerank, and rerank-file subcommands.
symbols.description: Defines --backend and --device globals plus the three subparsers, parses args, and invokes the matched command function.
